feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM | Implementation | CDM | SAP Launchpad Application
entry.content:
In a SAP Cloud ALM tenant, one of the landscapes has been set up with the change and deployment management module to manage transport requirements for SAP S4HANA, SAP CPI systems, and the SAP launchpad system. 

 

For above mentioned CPI and Launchpad system, the SAP cloud transport management system (CTMS) is already integrated into the SAP cloud alm tool via the SAP BTP cockpit.

CTS is enabled for S4HANA system.

 

I can view all the systems of above-mentioned sap applications in the landscape management of SAP cloud ALM administration Console. 

 

The system groups and deployment plans are in place. In system group for launchpad system we have tried with two products as SAP BTP cloud foundry environment  and SAP build work zone, standard edition

For the  SAP CPI we have selected product as SAP integration suite.

 

We can create features and add transport for the S/4HANA and CPI systems in the Features app.

However, when adding transport for the Launchpad system and selecting the 'Assign' button in the Features app, none of the transports of  Launchpad systems are getting listed. Rest of the systems S4HANA and CPI are listed, but the Launchpad system is not.  

 

Checked following :

Flag of “controlled by Solution Manager” is not set for Dev and UAT system, though for the prod system is set, but as of now not able to view the Dev or UAT in the list while assigning the transport.

Same CTMS is already leveraged for CPI systems, and no issues are observed nor encounter any errors for CPI transports.

In LMS of Cloud ALM, the attempt to create endpoint creation is throwing error stating endpoint creation is not required for SAP build work zone standard edition as none of the CALM use cases requires endpoint.

entry.title: Replacement of the transaction code in the effect of SAP Early Watch Alert (EWA) report
entry.content:
As part of the latest SAP Early Watch Alert (EWA) report, we have observed an alert regarding “Applications Being Replaced” in the Finance/PO system.
 
We request your support to review this finding and assess its potential impact on the S/4 landscape.
 
SAP EarlyWatch Alert Reports - SAP for Me
 
The following transactions are being used in daily Finance/PO activities. please propose an alternative approach to mitigate the use of these obsolete/deprecated transactions.
 
 
• WB03 – Obsolete – Plant Display
 
• F0932A – Deprecated – Profit Centers – Plan/Actual YTD
 
• F0938A – Deprecated – Profit Centers – Plan/Actual with Currency Translation

entry.title: Unable to import the wsdl properly
entry.content:
Hello Team,
We are migrating the interfaces from SAP PO to SAP CPI.
As we are migrating the flows/message mappings , we were unable to import the wsdl properly, as we are getting missing nodes[orange in colour]. 
Due to this, we are unable to complete the message mapping , which is blocking our development.

entry.title: How can we copy the mmap file for reusing it for other mapping in an iflow
entry.content:
Hi, 

There is a requirement in our project as there are 3 partners and 2 are using the same mapping and for third one we just need to do the changes in two or three fields and rest all fields mapping are same..so we need to copy the mmap file for the third partner and do the changes but we are not able to do so.. How can we able to do that?? Can you please provide us the inputs??

CPI URL:Integration Suite 

Iflow name: 

 

IF_OTC_I221_S4_to_TP_Invoice5.0_CustOrder_v1

entry.title: Automating Shipment Tracking Updates and Customer Notifications using SAP Integration Suite (CPI)
entry.content:
Introduction
In modern supply chain operations, customers expect timely updates regarding their shipments. This blog describes how SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) can be used to automate shipment tracking updates received from a third-party logistics provider and simultaneously notify customers via email. this will likely take 9–11 minutes to read end-to-end

Business Requirement

A third-party delivery partner, ECOM, receives shipment information from SAP. Once the shipment is processed, ECOM sends back shipment details including:

  • Tracking Number
  • Order Number
  • Shipment Status
  • Customer Name
  • Customer Email Address
  • Email Notification Flag

The requirement was to:

  1. Update the corresponding order information in SAP using an IDoc.
  2. Send an email notification to the customer containing the shipment tracking details.
  3. Ensure that customer notifications are sent only when the Email Notification Flag is enabled.

Solution Overview

The integration was developed using SAP Integration Suite – Cloud Integration (CPI).
Overview of the flow.

ECOM
↓ HTTPS (JSON)
SAP CPI
├─ IDoc → SAP
└─ Email → Customer

Source System :  ECOM sends shipment updates in JSON format through an HTTPS endpoint exposed by the CI iFlow.

Target Systems

  • SAP S4HANA via IDoc
  • Customer Email Inbox via Mail Adapter

Integration Flow Design:  The iFlow follows the below processing steps:

Step 1: HTTPS Sender Adapter
The shipment update is received from ECOM through an HTTPS endpoint in JSON format. you can always add an extra security by enabling csrf token protected here.

Step 2: JSON to XML Converter
The incoming JSON payload is converted into XML format to simplify downstream processing and mapping activities. I skipped adding XML root here because I am passing the root name in my source request. 

Step 3: Content Modifier
A Content Modifier is used to:

  • Set required header values using xpath expressions
  • Populate exchange properties for logging payload for example on or off.
  • Prepare values needed for routing and IDoc processing

Step 4: Groovy Script – Logging
A custom Groovy script is used to log the incoming payload and relevant message headers for monitoring and troubleshooting purposes.

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message

def Message processData(Message message) {
def body = message.getBody(String)

def properties = message.getProperties()
def logPayload = properties.get("LogPayload")

def messageLog = messageLogFactory.getMessageLog(message)
if (messageLog != null && logPayload == "true")
{
messageLog.addAttachmentAsString("Payload Log", body, "text/plain")
}

return message
}

Step 5: Router
The router evaluates business conditions, particularly the notification flag.
Typical routing conditions:

  • Update SAP system
  • Send customer email if EmailNotificationFlag = true
  • also a default route in case the input is not meant to send to SAP or mail.

Route 1 – SAP Update Processing

Step 6: Message Mapping
The XML payload is mapped to the required SAP IDoc SHPMNT structure.
Mapping includes fields such as:

  • Order Number
  • Tracking Number
  • Shipment Status
  • SAP Delivery  Number

Step 7: Groovy Script – Payload Logging
An additional Groovy script logs the transformed IDoc payload before transmission to SAP.

Step 8: Request Reply
The Request Reply step ensures synchronous communication with SAP and captures the response.

Step 9: IDoc Receiver Adapter
The generated IDoc is sent to SAP S4HANA to update shipment tracking information.

Route 2 – Customer Notification Processing

Step 10: Send Step
The notification branch is triggered when the Email Notification Flag is enabled.

Step 11: Mail Adapter
The Mail Adapter sends an email directly to the customer containing:

  • Customer Name
  • Order Number
  • Tracking Number
  • Shipment Status

Mail Body:

<p>Dear Customer ${header.CustomerName},</p>
<p><br />You Order ${header.OrderNumber} has been shipped. Your shipment arrive soon.</p>
<p>Tracking Number is: ${header.OrderTrackingNumber}. You can use the tracking number to monitor the delivery status of your shipment.</p>
<p>For further support please reach out to our customer support.</p>
<p>Thank you for choosing our services.</p>
<p>Regards,</p>
<p>Customer Service Team.</p>
Sample Json Payload:
{
"PlannedStartTime": "10-06-2026T00:00:00",
"PlannedEndTime": "14-06-2026T00:00:00",
"TourNumber": "415487",
"OrderTrackingNumber": "TN12345678900",
"TransportOrderNumber": "2002121364",
"OrderStatus": "Shipped",
"SAPflag": true,
"Information": "Shipment created",
"ExternalNumber": "415487",
"Customer": {
"CustomerEmail": "customer@example.com",
"CustomerName": "John Doe",
"EmailNotificationFlag": true
}
}

Example email output content:

Dear Customer,
You Order 415487 has been shipped. Your shipment arrive soon.

Tracking Number is: TN12345678900, You can use the tracking number to monitor the delivery status of your shipment.

Thank you for choosing our services.
Regards,
Customer Service Team

Benefits of the Solution

  • Real-time shipment tracking updates
  • Automated SAP order synchronization
  • Improved customer communication
  • Reduced manual intervention
  • Better shipment visibility across systems

Conclusion
Using SAP Integration Suite, it is possible to efficiently orchestrate shipment updates from external logistics providers while simultaneously updating SAP business documents and keeping customers informed through automated email notifications. This approach improves operational efficiency and enhances the overall customer experience by providing timely and accurate shipment information.

Lessons Learned and Common Errors During Testing

During the development and testing of this integration scenario, several issues were encountered. The following troubleshooting notes may help others implementing similar solutions.

1. HTTP Status 403 – Forbidden

Error

HTTP Status 403 – Forbidden

Root Cause: The user invoking the HTTPS endpoint did not have the required authorization to send messages to the deployed integration flow.
Resolution: Ensure that the user calling the CPI endpoint has the required role assignment.
Required role: Once the role was assigned and the user re-authenticated, the HTTPS call was successfully processed.

MessagingSend

2. UnknownHostException while Calling SAP IDoc Service

Error

com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException:
An internal server error occured:
UnknownHostException invoking
http://s4d:8010/sap/bc/srt/idoc?sap-client=120: s4d

Root Cause: SAP Integration Suite was unable to resolve or reach the backend SAP system.
Resolution: Verify the following configuration steps:

  1. The SAP backend system is correctly configured in SAP Cloud Connector.
  2. The virtual host and virtual port are maintained correctly.
  3. The exact IDoc service path is exposed and allowed in Cloud Connector.
  4. Connectivity tests between SAP Integration Suite and Cloud Connector are successful.
  5. The IDoc service is activated and registered in the SAP backend system.

After correcting the Cloud Connector configuration and exposing the required path, the IDoc communication worked successfully

3. SMTP Connectivity Error while Sending Email

Error

com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException:
An internal server error occured:
Could not connect to SMTP host: smtp.gmail.com,
port: 465, response: -1

Root Cause: The mail adapter could not establish a secure connection with Gmail's SMTP server.
Resolution: Verify the following:

  1. Correct SMTP host and port configuration.
  2. Appropriate authentication method configured in the Mail Adapter.
  3. Gmail security settings allow SMTP access.
  4. App Passwords or OAuth authentication are configured according to Google's latest security requirements.
  5. TLS/SSL settings match the selected SMTP port.

Once the correct Gmail security configuration was implemented, email notifications were delivered successfully.

4. Malformed JSON Payload

Error

com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException:
An internal server error occured:
Use JsonReader.setLenient(true) to accept malformed JSON
at line 11 column 20 path $.TourData.Tour[0].SAPflag

Root Cause: The incoming JSON payload contained invalid syntax in the SAPflag field. In this case, the issue was caused by incorrect input data during testing.
Resolution: Review the JSON payload and validate it using a JSON validator before sending it to SAP Integration Suite.

One thing worth highlighting is how clearly SAP Integration Suite identifies payload issues. The error message not only reported the parsing failure but also pinpointed the exact location of the problematic field:

$.TourData.Tour[0].SAPflag

This significantly reduced troubleshooting time and made root cause identification straightforward.

Final Thoughts

While the implementation itself was relatively straightforward, most challenges were related to connectivity, authorization, and payload validation. SAP Integration Suite provides detailed monitoring and Message Processing Logs (MPL), which greatly simplify issue analysis and resolution.

Hopefully, these lessons learned will help other developers accelerate their implementations and reduce troubleshooting efforts when integrating external logistics providers with SAP systems.

entry.title: AS2 Partner ID Reuse Across Multiple Partner Profiles in SAP CPI TPM
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I’m fairly new to SAP CPI Trading Partner Management (TPM) and I’ve run into a situation I don’t know how to solve.

  • I have 10 partners who all use the same AS2 Partner ID (SIBSERVICE1005), the same AS2 connection, and the same certificate.

  • In TPM, the AS2 Partner ID must be unique per Partner Profile, so CPI doesn’t allow me to assign SIBSERVICE1005 to more than one profile.

  • If I change the AS2 ID to keep them unique, the actual process becomes incorrect, because all partners technically use the same AS2 ID and certificate in reality.

Since I’m still exploring TPM, I’d really appreciate guidance from the community:

  1. How can I configure TPM so that multiple partners can share the same AS2 ID and certificate?

  2. Is the recommended approach to create one Partner Profile with the shared AS2 ID and then manage partner‑specific logic at the Agreement level?

  3. Are there any best practices for handling certificate reuse across multiple agreements or partners in this scenario?

Any guidance or examples from your experience would be very helpful.

Thanks in advance,

entry.title: SAP HCM to Third Party Integration (LIve)
entry.content: SAP HCM master data needs to be updated in our third-party systems.  How can this be achieved in real time? We are not able to find any time stamp or event triggered at the time of data change/update in infotypes? What is the SAP recommended approach for real-time HR master data update propagation to external systems? We are unable to find an SAP note.

entry.title: Disabling Father's and Husband's field from dependent
entry.content: In the dependent , We are getting fathers and husbands name from personnel information. Is there any way to remove only these two  fields from the dependent.

entry.title: Issue on one job code mapping with multiple job roles.
entry.content:
Hi Team,
We have a query regarding JPB - job code mapping to multiple roles. we were configuring Job Code Association with Multiple Job Roles, followed the process which is mentioned in SAP Notes. WE created a custom field as custom_09  in manage business configuration and making it editable then saving it we are getting the error and same in configure object Definition. Please check the attached screenshot reagarding error.

entry.title: Support for Partner Directory On Edge
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

We thought the long awaited support for partner directory on Edge is finally delivered , below was the announcement for reference .  This community blog is offline since friday.

Not sure if the functionality was not released yet or what happened

https://community.sap.com/t5/integration-blog-posts/run-pipeline-for-cloud-integration-on-edge-integration-cell-with-partner/ba-p/14369882

entry.title: Issues with case sensitivity with jdbc batch operations when writing to SAP Hana Cloud DB.
entry.content:
Hello.  When using batch operations of an insert statement my column name is all lower case 'flowname' but the error comes back as "FLOWNAME" not found in your database.

This is the content modifier.  Flowname is all lower case.

This is the error, saying FLOWNAME is not found.

This is the database shema, showing the column is all lower case. 

I'm not sure why my content modifier is correct with all lower 'flowname' but in the runtime this fails over to all upper case 'FLOWNAME'.  Then the error make sense because SAP Hana Cloud DB is case sensitive.  Q - how do maintain my xml element called flowname be all lower case?  Is there an setting i have missed or is my batch operation wrong?

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/batch-payload-and-operation

Thanks.

entry.title: Events via Cloud Integration
entry.content:
hi all,

we have two cases where we are sending Events fe.

entry.title: Capability of Integration Suite to handle large amount of requests
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I have a question regarding capability of Integration Suite to handle a large amount or requests per second. I need to process around 25k requests for billing, so IS sends to ERP which handles this process. But I need to control this flow, retries, exeptions, etc. by Integration Suite.

Can IS handle lots of request/second? I need around 14.2 request/min. For each request I need traceability. Does IS manage something like a staging table, or how does it process each request, exceptions, retries, etc?  

Suppose there is something like 40k transactions I need to process per day. Do you think IS could handle this volume?

entry.title: Payload Corruption while posting pdf attachment via APIM.
entry.content:
Hi All,

I need to post attachments (PDF/TXT), from a 3rd party to S4 API "API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV" via APIM, 

While creating the request to fetch token the original payload in body is getting corrupted and not reaching to S4.

I have tried storing the payload in a variable and used that to post the request still the payload is not reaching S4.

API Management

entry.title: could not fetch content packages in SAP Integration Suite under design mode.
entry.content: I am experiencing an error message "could not fetch content packages" in SAP Integration Suite when using design mode. I need assistance to understand and resolve this issue.

entry.title: Azure OAuth Integration Failure
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are encountering issues while configuring OAuth 2.0 authentication for Microsoft Graph API integration in SAP Cloud Integration (CPI).

The integration is intended to enable reading emails from an Office365 mailbox using Azure AD–based authentication (Client Credentials / OAuth 2.0).

PFA the error screenshot for the same.

Regards,
Yashashree

entry.title: Integrate SAP S/4HANA on-premises with ActiveMQ on-premises using Edge Integration Cell
entry.content:
Hi,

We work with a private on-premises version of SAP S/4HANA and we would like to exchange events between that instance and some ActiveMQ queues (on-premises too).

The condition is that the messages/events stay on-premises and don't go outside on any hyperscalers' infrastructure.

We have a Kubernetes infrastructure on-premises too.

It seems that with such landscape it should be possible to configure a runtime with Edge Integration Cell on-premises, add a JSM or AMQP Adapter in the design of the intregration and then exchange events between the EIC and SAP S/4HANA.

Is it correct ?

Thanks

entry.title: Reprocessing Retry Messages in SAP CPI
entry.content:
We observed that when the SAP Cloud Connector becomes unavailable, messages sent from SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) to the on-premise system fail and are moved to the retry queue based on the configured retry mechanism.

Once the Cloud Connector is restored and connectivity is re-established, the failed/retried messages can be reprocessed from CPI Monitoring.

entry.title: Reprocessing of Retried Messages in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

whenever we have the failure of sender system massages not being delivered and messages failed in SAP CPI can you please let us know the option of failed message in SAP CPI

entry.title: SAP guide for release pipeline for SAP IS to KAFKA
entry.content:
Hello SAP, 

We are currently trying to create an integration between SAP IS and KAFKA through KaTe adapter. We would like to create release pipeline which is backed by automated unit tests. We are ok to leverage gitlab.  

Can you please help us to know more about the process of if SAP IS allows this type of release pipeline or not.

Thanks,

Dhruba
